ABVX.headline: What's Going On With Abivax Stock On Thursday?
ABVX.description: Abivax stock rose 3.46% on Thursday despite concerns following the release of Phase 3 trial data for obefazimod in ulcerative colitis. While the drug met primary endpoints with remission rates exceeding 50%, reported cancer cases during the trial have raised safety concerns among investors. The stock trades below its moving averages, with momentum indicators suggesting weakness.

ABVX.headline: This Stock Is Up 1,000% Over the Past Year. Is There More Upside Ahead?
ABVX.description: Abivax, a French biotech company, saw its stock surge 1,000% over the past year following strong phase 3 results for obefazimod, a non-immunosuppressant ulcerative colitis treatment. However, recent maintenance trial data revealed safety concerns including cancer cases, causing a sharp stock decline. The author argues the market may be overreacting, noting that cancer cases were mostly in older patients and may be unrelated to the drug, while obefazimod still shows strong efficacy and oral conve

HPE.headline: The Great AI Server Rotation Puts Hewlett Packard and Super Micro in Focus
HPE.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise's strong Q2 2026 earnings and record $5.9 billion AI backlog signal a shift in AI infrastructure adoption beyond hyperscalers to enterprise and edge deployments. This structural pivot challenges Super Micro Computer's dominance, as legacy OEMs capture market share. HPE trades at an attractive 15.6x forward P/E with accelerating cash flow, while Super Micro pivots toward edge computing to defend its position against increased competition.

HPE.headline: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Shares Surge on AI Demand. Is It Too Late to Buy the Stock?
HPE.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise reported strong Q2 results with 40% revenue growth and doubled adjusted EPS, driven by AI infrastructure demand. The stock has tripled over the past year and trades at a 20x forward P/E. While the company benefits from the AI infrastructure boom, analyst concerns about potential demand pull-forward and the stock's already significant gains suggest there may be better opportunities in the AI sector.

RDW.headline: Why Redwire Stock Is Skyrocketing Higher Today
RDW.description: Redwire stock surged 18% after securing a contract with Astrobiome Space to grow strawberries and test soil enhancement products on the International Space Station using its new commercial Greenhouse system. The deal highlights Redwire's engineering expertise as a 'picks and shovels' supplier to the space industry. However, the stock remains volatile and dilutive to shareholders, with concerns about margin pressures from recent acquisitions, though Q1 2025 showed improved gross margins of 26.6%

RDW.headline: Space Stocks Bleed: Redwire, Momentus Down More Than 20% As SpaceX IPO Fatigue Hits
RDW.description: The space sector experienced significant losses this week as multiple space stocks declined sharply. Virgin Galactic's short squeeze collapsed, dropping 30.58%, while Redwire fell 24.22% and Momentus declined 22.14%. The selloff was driven by SpaceX IPO fatigue after the company reduced its valuation target from $2 trillion to $1.75 trillion, with pricing set for June 11 at $135 per share.

MRVL.headline: The Hidden Nvidia Trade Nobody on Wall Street Is Talking About
MRVL.description: Nvidia is making strategic investments in AI infrastructure companies including Lumentum, Coherent, and Marvell Technology to secure supply chains and expand its AI hardware ecosystem. These investments in optical networking and custom AI processors position Nvidia to maintain dominance in the AI semiconductor space while potentially generating profitable returns.

MRVL.headline: S&P 500 Selloff Looks More Like Rotation Than Market Breakdown
MRVL.description: A stronger-than-expected May jobs report (172,000 payrolls vs. 80,000-105,000 forecast) triggered a market rotation rather than a broad selloff. The strong labor data pushed Treasury yields to 4.54%, reducing rate-cut expectations and hitting rate-sensitive tech stocks. While the Nasdaq fell 1.13% and semiconductor stocks cratered, the Russell 2000 surged 1.45% as money rotated into cyclicals and small caps. The Dow held near record highs, indicating broadening market participation rather than a

MRVL.headline: Nvidia Sends a Message With RTX Spark—This Is What It Says
MRVL.description: Nvidia unveiled the RTX Spark, a superchip combining Blackwell RTX graphics, Arm CPU cores, and an NPU for personal computers, marking the company's entry into the PC market. The chip delivers petaflop AI computing power and unified memory for local AI applications. Analysts view this as part of Nvidia's broader strategy to dominate AI across all layers—data centers, edge, PCs, and IoT—with strong revenue growth expected and price targets reaching $500 within 12 months.

MRVL.headline: NVIDIA CEO Says AI's Future Isn't Just Copper
MRVL.description: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang challenges the popular copper supercycle narrative, arguing that AI infrastructure will use a hybrid approach combining copper and optical technologies. While copper demand will grow, engineering innovations like 800-volt systems and fiber optics will reduce copper intensity per data center. The article highlights that technological evolution and infrastructure constraints limit unlimited copper demand growth.

MRVL.headline: Why the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) Jumped 23% in May
MRVL.description: The iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) surged 23% in May driven by strong earnings reports, memory chip shortages expected through 2029, and growing demand for CPUs in AI inference. Top holdings Micron and AMD led gains, though a Broadcom earnings miss and concerns about Google diversifying away from custom chips caused a 6% pullback on Thursday.

NVTS.headline: Why Navitas Stock Plummeted Today
NVTS.description: Navitas Semiconductor stock fell 18.2% on Friday following a broader market sell-off triggered by stronger-than-expected May jobs data. The robust employment numbers (172,000 jobs added vs. 80,000 forecast) raised investor concerns that the Federal Reserve will pursue interest rate hikes to combat accelerating inflation, pressuring growth-dependent AI stocks like Navitas.

NVTS.headline: Navitas: Nvidia’s AI Power Broker?
NVTS.description: Navitas Semiconductor has surged over 60% in May 2026 following its integration into NVIDIA's MGX ecosystem with an innovative 800V-to-6V power delivery board that eliminates legacy conversion stages and achieves 97.5% efficiency. The company's AI infrastructure revenue grew 50% quarter-over-quarter, though a $6 billion valuation and 18% short float create both opportunity and risk. Key concerns include execution timelines, insider selling near peak prices, and analyst caution about prolonged ad
